question_text: Read the passage given below. What emotion does the child seem to feel as the procession moves on without him?

answer_key.explanation: The narrator describes the child as suddenly lost, looking around for parents who are no longer in sight.
common_mistakes: Reading the wonder of the fair as joy rather than fear.
answer_key.related_concept: Inferring emotion from a narrative extract

visual.caption: Numbered extract from a Moments short story.
visual.altText: A four-line numbered passage from a short story describing a child separated from his parents at a fair.

spec.title: Lost at the fair
spec.source: Adapted from a Moments short story

lines.text: The procession moved on but the child stood still.
There were men on horses, kings and dancers in the parade,
but the child saw none of them now —
his parents were nowhere in the crowd.
